element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Walking and jumping without helmets; multiple speeds available (crawl, walk, run) with different acceleration curves
Bouncing on enemies grants speed boost; can chain multiple bounces; different enemies have different bounce properties
Each mask grants unique abilities and varying hit points; includes SkyCutter, MicroMax, Red Stealth, Cyclone, Berzerker, Maniaxe
1-block height/width; can climb walls; fits between horizontal spikes; zig-zag climbing in narrow tunnels; increased mid-air jump control
High horizontal speed; near-flying with gravity modulation; difficult control; cannot stand still; turns slowly; requires forward/jump input for reduced gravity
Melee combat with sword; variable striking speed based on timing; can attack roughly every 13 frames normally
Repeated C-button presses enable flying; slower than running normally but can preserve speed from falls/bounces
Axe throwing with cooldown (18 frames standard); secondary weapon; not required for main path
Ice accelerates movement (~0:4 per frame); rubber reduces max speed; spikes are obstacles; cannon blocks shoot projectiles
Warp within same level, to other levels, or to alternate paths; creates non-linear progression choices
Resource-gated special abilities requiring diamonds to activate; form-specific
Primary objective; reaching flag progresses to next level; not all levels contain flags

typical_relief_triggers: Finding safe platforms after dangerous jumps
Obtaining mask with favorable form for upcoming section
Landing safely after bouncing on enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Ceiling-low sections limiting jump height options
SkyCutter form sections requiring high-speed control
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Variable based on selected form; base Kid is moderate pace; MicroMax encourages constant jumping; SkyCutter enables high-speed runs with control difficulty; overall progression designed for repeated attempts through non-linear paths

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Teleporters warp to alternate paths and hidden levels; non-linear progression allows player choice in route selection
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Masks obtained from pickups throughout levels grant access to previously inaccessible areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic platforming and enemy-bouncing mechanics in first two levels; emphasis on building speed management skills
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of multiple forms with distinct control properties (MicroMax climbing, Cyclone flight); mixed platforming and form-specific puzzle sections
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-speed SkyCutter sections requiring sustained precision control; complex multi-form level designs; Final Marathon as capstone challenge
notable_spikes: SkyCutter form introduction - high horizontal speed difficult to control
Final Marathon level - two-block gaps at high speed requiring maintained momentum
Double-inversion bugs with SkyCutter creating sine-wave motion challenges
Sections requiring form-specific knowledge (MicroMax narrow tunnels, SkyCutter gravity management)

notable_level_patterns.description: Vertical or narrow sections utilizing MicroMax's wall-cling ability; zig-zag pattern more efficient than single-wall ascent
Long horizontal sections requiring maintained momentum and precise jumping at speed; SkyCutter form primary use case
Sequences rewarding repeated bouncing on enemies for speed accumulation; early game emphasis
Sections designed around particular form abilities; Red Stealth sword striking, Cyclone flight maintenance, etc.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bagel Brothers (Red Stealth striking), Under Skull Mountain 1 (Cyclone speed preservation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels containing multiple teleporters creating non-linear path options; increases replayability and creates player choice tension
Sections emphasizing ice acceleration, rubber deceleration, or spike avoidance; tests mastery of form-specific movement properties

sources_used: tasvideos - Kid Chameleon mechanics breakdown with character form specifics
wikipedia - gameplay overview, level structure, form abilities
